𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty-Four — Part Three

Divrei HaYamim Bet

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET

Covenant History — Shelomoh and the Early Kings of Yehudah

Chapters 1 through 18

If My People Humble Themselves and Pray and Seek My Face — The Eyes of YHWH Range Throughout the Earth — YHWH Is With You While You Are With Him — As YHWH Lives What My Creator Says That I Will Speak

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Orit Ge'ez — Ethiopian Complete Canon

Masoretic Text

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — SHELOMOH — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 9

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 1 Shelomoh Went to Givon — Creator Appeared to Shelomoh — Ask What I Shall Give You — Give Me Wisdom and Knowledge — Because You Have Asked This — Shelomoh's Great Wealth

1 Shelomoh the son of Dawid established himself in his kingdom, and YHWH his Creator was with him and made him exceedingly great.

2 Shelomoh spoke to all Yisra'EL, to the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, to the judges, and to every leader in all Yisra'EL, the heads of fathers' houses.

3 And Shelomoh, and all the assembly with him, went to the high place that was at Givon, for the tent of meeting of Creator, which Mosheh the servant of YHWH had made in the wilderness, was there.

4 But Dawid had brought up the ark of Creator from Kiryat-Ye'arim to the place that Dawid had prepared for it, for he had pitched a tent for it in Yerushalayim.

5 Moreover, the bronze altar that Betzalel the son of Uri, son of Khur, had made, was there before the tabernacle of YHWH. And Shelomoh and the assembly sought it out.

6 And Shelomoh went up there to the bronze altar before YHWH, which was at the tent of meeting, and offered a thousand burnt offerings on it.

7 In that night Creator appeared to Shelomoh, and said to him, Ask what I shall give you.

8 And Shelomoh said to Creator, You have shown great and steadfast love to Dawid my father, and have made me king in his place.

9 O YHWH Creator, let your word to Dawid my father be now fulfilled, for you have made me king over a people as numerous as the dust of the earth.

10 Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people, for who can govern this people of yours, which is so great?

11 Creator said to Shelomoh, Because this was in your heart, and you have not asked for possessions, wealth, honor, or the life of those who hate you, and have not even asked for long life, but have asked for wisdom and knowledge for yourself that you may govern my people over whom I have made you king,

12 Wisdom and knowledge are granted to you. I will also give you riches, possessions, and honor, such as none of the kings had who were before you, and none after you shall have the like.

13 So Shelomoh came from the high place at Givon, from before the tent of meeting, to Yerushalayim. And he reigned over Yisra'EL.

14 Shelomoh gathered together chariots and horsemen. He had 1,400 chariots and 12,000 horsemen, whom he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Yerushalayim.

15 And the king made silver and gold as common in Yerushalayim as stone, and he made cedar as plentiful as the sycamore of the Shephelah.

16 And Shelomoh's import of horses was from Egypt and Kue, and the king's traders received them from Kue at a price.

17 They imported a chariot from Egypt for 600 shekels of silver, and a horse for 150. Likewise through them these were exported to all the kings of the Hittites and the kings of Syria.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities for Divrei HaYamim Bet. Chapter 1 establishes the covenant theological foundation of the Shelomoh narrative: the covenant king who begins his reign by seeking YHWH at Givon receives the covenant divine offer — ask what I shall give you — and asks for covenant wisdom and knowledge rather than covenant personal covenant advantage. The covenant request for covenant wisdom for the sake of covenant governance produces covenant all other blessings additionally.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 10 — give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people, for who can govern this people of yours, which is so great? The covenant humility of the covenant request: Shelomoh does not ask for covenant personal covenant benefit but for the covenant capacity to serve the covenant community. The covenant motivation for the covenant request — the covenant greatness of the covenant people — is the covenant foundation of the covenant answer.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 11-12 — because this was in your heart and you have not asked for possessions, wealth, or honor — wisdom and knowledge are granted to you, and I will also give you riches and honor such as none of the kings had before you. The covenant principle of covenant priority: the covenant king who seeks covenant wisdom for covenant service receives covenant wealth as a covenant additional covenant gift.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — in that night Creator appeared to Shelomoh and said, Ask what I shall give you. The covenant divine invitation that opens the covenant Shelomoh narrative: the covenant blank-check covenant offer to the covenant new king. The covenant king's covenant response to this covenant extraordinary covenant invitation reveals the covenant character of the covenant king more than any covenant other single act: Shelomoh asks for covenant wisdom to serve, not covenant wealth to accumulate.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 2 Shelomoh Purposed to Build a House — He Sent to Khuram King of Tzor — The House I Am to Build Will Be Great — Who Am I That I Should Build Him a House — Khuram Answered

1 Now Shelomoh purposed to build a house for the name of YHWH, and a royal palace for himself.

2 And Shelomoh assigned 70,000 men to bear burdens and 80,000 to quarry in the hill country, and 3,600 to oversee them.

3 And Shelomoh sent word to Khuram the king of Tzor: As you dealt with Dawid my father and sent him cedar to build himself a house to dwell in, so deal with me.

4 Behold, I am about to build a house for the name of YHWH my Creator and dedicate it to him for the burning of incense of sweet spices before him, and for the regular showbread, and for burnt offerings morning and evening, on the Shabbatot and the new moons and the appointed feasts of YHWH our Creator, as ordained forever for Yisra'EL.

5 The house that I am to build will be great, for our Creator is greater than all gods.

6 But who is able to build him a house, since heaven, even highest heaven, cannot contain him? Who am I to build a house for him, except as a place to make offerings before him?

7 So now send me a man skilled to work in gold, silver, bronze, and iron, and in purple, crimson, and blue fabrics, trained also in engraving, to be with the skilled workers who are with me in Yehudah and Yerushalayim, whom Dawid my father provided.

8 Send me also cedar, cypress, and algum timber from Lebanon, for I know that your servants know how to cut timber in Lebanon. And my servants will be with your servants,

9 To prepare timber for me in abundance, for the house I am to build will be great and wonderful.

10 I will give for your servants, the woodcutters who cut timber, 20,000 cors of crushed wheat, 20,000 cors of barley, 20,000 baths of wine, and 20,000 baths of oil.

11 Then Khuram the king of Tzor answered in a letter that he sent to Shelomoh, Because YHWH loves his people, he has made you king over them.

12 Khuram also said, Blessed be YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL, who made heaven and earth, who has given King Dawid a wise son, who has discretion and understanding, who will build a temple for YHWH and a royal palace for himself.

13 Now I have sent a skilled man, endowed with understanding, Khuram-Avi,

14 The son of a woman of the daughters of Dan, and his father was a man of Tzor. He is trained to work in gold, silver, bronze, iron, stone, and wood, and in purple, blue, and crimson fabrics and fine linen, and to do all sorts of engraving and execute any design that may be assigned him, with your craftsmen, the craftsmen of my lord, Dawid your father.

15 Now therefore the wheat and barley, oil and wine, of which my lord has spoken, let him send to his servants.

16 And we will cut whatever timber you need from Lebanon and bring it to you in rafts by sea to Yapho, so that you may take it up to Yerushalayim.

17 Then Shelomoh counted all the resident aliens who were in the land of Yisra'EL, after the census of them that Dawid his father had taken, and there were found 153,600.

18 Seventy thousand of them he assigned to bear burdens, 80,000 to quarry in the hill country, and 3,600 as overseers to make the people work.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 2 is the covenant treaty negotiation between Shelomoh and Khuram of Tzor for the covenant materials and skilled covenant craftsmen to build the covenant temple. The covenant acknowledgment of Khuram — a pagan king — that YHWH loves his people and made Shelomoh king — verse 11 — is the covenant compiler's statement that the covenant temple-building project is recognized even by the covenant nations as a covenant divine covenant initiative.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 5-6 — the house I am to build will be great, for our Creator is greater than all gods. But who is able to build him a house, since heaven, even highest heaven, cannot contain him? The covenant theological paradox of the covenant temple: the covenant building is covenant great because YHWH is great — but no covenant building can contain YHWH who exceeds all covenant created space. The covenant temple is a covenant place for covenant offerings before YHWH, not a covenant container for YHWH.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 14 — Khuram-Avi, the son of a woman of the daughters of Dan, his father was a man of Tzor. The covenant craftsman sent by Khuram to build the covenant temple has a covenant mixed ancestry — covenant Israelite mother of Dan's tribe, covenant Tyrian father — paralleling Betzalel who built the covenant tabernacle. The covenant temple, like the covenant tabernacle, is built with covenant skills that transcend covenant ethnic covenant boundaries.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12 — Blessed be YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL, who made heaven and earth, who has given King Dawid a wise son. The covenant pagan king Khuram's covenant blessing of YHWH is the covenant diplomatic acknowledgment that the covenant building project has a covenant theological dimension beyond covenant commercial covenant exchange. The covenant foreign king recognizes the covenant divine covenant hand in the covenant succession from Dawid to Shelomoh.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 3 Shelomoh Began to Build — On Mount Moriyah — The Dimensions of the Temple — The Vestibule — The Most Holy Place — The Cherubim — The Veil

1 Then Shelomoh began to build the house of YHWH in Yerushalayim on Mount Moriyah, where YHWH had appeared to Dawid his father, at the place that Dawid had appointed, on the threshing floor of Ornan the Yevusite.

2 He began to build in the second month of the fourth year of his reign.

3 These are Solomon's measurements for building the house of Creator: the length, in cubits of the old standard, was sixty cubits, and the breadth twenty cubits.

4 The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits long, equal to the width of the house, and its height was 120 cubits. He overlaid it on the inside with pure gold.

5 The nave he lined with cypress and covered it with fine gold and made palms and chains on it.

6 He adorned the house with settings of precious stones. The gold was gold of Parvaim.

7 So he lined the house with gold — its beams, its thresholds, its walls, and its doors — and he carved cherubim on the walls.

8 And he made the Most Holy Place. Its length, corresponding to the breadth of the house, was twenty cubits, and its breadth was twenty cubits. He overlaid it with 600 talents of fine gold.

9 The weight of gold for the nails was fifty shekels. And he overlaid the upper chambers with gold.

10 In the Most Holy Place he made two cherubim of wood and overlaid them with gold.

11 The wings of the cherubim together extended twenty cubits: one wing of the one, of five cubits, touched the wall of the house, and its other wing, of five cubits, touched the wing of the other cherub.

12 And of this cherub, one wing, of five cubits, touched the wall of the house, and the other wing, also of five cubits, was joined to the wing of the first cherub.

13 The wings of these cherubim extended twenty cubits. The cherubim stood on their feet, facing the nave.

14 And he made the veil of blue and purple and crimson fabrics and fine linen, and he worked cherubim on it.

15 In front of the house he made two pillars thirty-five cubits high, with a capital of five cubits on the top of each.

16 He made chains like a necklace and put them on the tops of the pillars, and he made a hundred pomegranates and put them on the chains.

17 He set up the pillars in front of the temple, one on the south, the other on the north. The one on the south he called Yakhin, and the one on the north Bo'az.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 3 is the covenant architectural record of the covenant temple construction — the covenant realization of the covenant plan that YHWH revealed to Dawid and Dawid transmitted to Shelomoh. The covenant theological geography of verse 1 is the covenant foundation: Mount Moriyah — the covenant mountain of Avraham's covenant near-sacrifice of Yitzkhak — is identified as the covenant site of the covenant temple.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 1 — Shelomoh began to build the house of YHWH in Yerushalayim on Mount Moriyah, where YHWH had appeared to Dawid his father, at the place that Dawid had appointed, on the threshing floor of Ornan the Yevusite. The covenant triple covenant identification of the covenant site: the covenant mountain of Avraham, the covenant vision of Dawid, and the covenant purchase of Ornan. The covenant site carries covenant history from Bereshit to the covenant present.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 3 in full. The covenant pillars Yakhin and Bo'az — verse 17 — mark the covenant entrance to the covenant temple. Yakhin means he will establish and Bo'az means in him is strength — the covenant names of the covenant pillars announce the covenant theological program of the covenant temple: YHWH establishes and YHWH is the covenant strength of the covenant community that enters through these covenant pillars.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — Mount Moriyah. The covenant identification of the covenant temple site with the covenant mountain of Avraham's binding of Yitzkhak — Bereshit 22:2 — is the covenant most significant covenant geographic covenant statement in Divrei HaYamim. The covenant mountain of covenant near-sacrifice becomes the covenant mountain of covenant worship: the covenant altar that was prevented at Moriyah is established in the covenant temple on the same covenant mountain. Covenant history circles back to its covenant origin.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 4 He Made an Altar of Bronze — The Bronze Sea — The Ten Lavers — The Ten Lampstands — The Tables — The Courts — All the Vessels of the House

1 He made an altar of bronze, twenty cubits long and twenty cubits wide and ten cubits high.

2 Then he made the sea of cast metal. It was round, ten cubits from brim to brim, and five cubits high, and a line of thirty cubits measured its circumference.

3 Under it were figures of gourds, for ten cubits, compassing the sea all around. The gourds were in two rows, cast with it when it was cast.

4 It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east. The sea was set on them, and all their rear parts were inward.

5 Its thickness was a handbreadth. And its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily. It held 3,000 baths.

6 He also made ten lavers in which to wash, and set five on the south side, and five on the north side. In these they were to rinse off what was used for the burnt offering, and the sea was for the priests to wash in.

7 And he made ten golden lampstands as prescribed, and set them in the temple, five on the south side and five on the north.

8 He also made ten tables and placed them in the temple, five on the south side and five on the north. And he made a hundred basins of gold.

9 He made the court of the priests and the great court and doors for the court and overlaid their doors with bronze.

10 And he set the sea at the southeast corner of the house.

11 Khuram also made the pots, the shovels, and the basins. So Khuram finished the work that he did for King Shelomoh on the house of Creator:

12 The two pillars, the bowls, and the two capitals on the top of the pillars, and the two latticeworks to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the top of the pillars,

13 And the 400 pomegranates for the two latticeworks, two rows of pomegranates for each latticework, to cover the two bowls of the capitals that were on the pillars.

14 He made the stands also, and the lavers on the stands,

15 And the one sea, and the twelve oxen underneath it.

16 The pots, the shovels, the forks, and all the equipment for these Khuram-Avi made of burnished bronze for King Shelomoh for the house of YHWH.

17 In the plain of the Yarden the king cast them, in the clay ground between Sukkot and Tzeredah.

18 Shelomoh made all these things in great quantities, so that the weight of the bronze was not determined.

19 So Shelomoh made all the vessels that were in the house of Creator: the golden altar, the tables for the showbread,

20 The lampstands and their lamps of pure gold to burn before the inner sanctuary, as prescribed,

21 The flowers, the lamps, and the tongs, of purest gold,

22 The snuffers, basins, dishes for incense, and fire pans, of pure gold, and the sockets of the temple, for the inner doors to the Most Holy Place and for the doors of the nave of the temple were of gold.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 4 is the covenant inventory of the covenant temple furnishings — the covenant objects of covenant worship whose covenant materials, covenant dimensions, and covenant placement are covenant precisely documented. The covenant bronze sea of verse 2-5 — the covenant massive laver supported by twelve covenant bronze oxen — is the covenant grandest covenant single object in the covenant temple court.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 6 — the lavers were for rinsing off what was used for the burnt offering, and the sea was for the priests to wash in. The covenant function of the covenant temple's covenant water structures: the covenant lavers for covenant ritual covenant purity of covenant offerings, the covenant sea for the covenant priests' covenant purification. Covenant worship requires covenant ritual covenant cleansing at every covenant level.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 4 in full. The covenant precise measurement and covenant detailed description of every covenant temple furnishing is the covenant compiler's testimony that every covenant detail of the covenant worship space matters. The covenant architectural covenant precision of Chapters 3-4 reflects the covenant conviction that the covenant dwelling place of YHWH's covenant name deserves covenant maximum covenant human covenant attention and covenant craftsmanship.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18 — Shelomoh made all these things in great quantities, so that the weight of the bronze was not determined. The covenant deliberately indeterminate measurement: the covenant compiler acknowledges that the covenant scale of the covenant bronze work exceeded covenant normal covenant accounting. The covenant temple's covenant materials are covenant beyond ordinary covenant measure — a covenant statement about the covenant extraordinary nature of the covenant enterprise.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 5 All the Work Was Finished — The Ark Brought Into the Temple — The Levitical Singers — Praise YHWH For He Is Good — A Cloud Filled the House — The Priests Could Not Stand to Minister

1 Thus all the work that Shelomoh did for the house of YHWH was finished. And Shelomoh brought in the things that Dawid his father had dedicated, and stored the silver, the gold, and all the vessels in the treasuries of the house of Creator.

2 Then Shelomoh assembled the elders of Yisra'EL and all the heads of the tribes, the leaders of the fathers' houses of the people of Yisra'EL, in Yerushalayim, to bring up the ark of the covenant of YHWH out of the city of Dawid, which is Tziyon.

3 And all the men of Yisra'EL assembled before the king at the feast that is in the seventh month.

4 And all the elders of Yisra'EL came, and the Levites took up the ark.

5 And they brought up the ark, the tent of meeting, and all the holy vessels that were in the tent. The Levitical priests brought them up.

6 And King Shelomoh and all the congregation of Yisra'EL, who had assembled before him, were before the ark, sacrificing so many sheep and oxen that they could not be counted or numbered.

7 So the priests brought the ark of the covenant of YHWH to its place, into the inner sanctuary of the house, to the Most Holy Place, underneath the wings of the cherubim.

8 The cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark, so that the cherubim made a covering above the ark and its poles.

9 And the poles were so long that the ends of the poles were seen from the Holy Place before the inner sanctuary, but they could not be seen from outside. And they are there to this day.

10 There was nothing in the ark except the two tablets that Mosheh put there at Khoreiv, where YHWH made a covenant with the people of Yisra'EL, when they came out of Egypt.

11 And when the priests came out of the Holy Place — for all the priests who were present had consecrated themselves, without regard to their divisions —

12 And all the Levitical singers, Asaf, Heman, and Yedutun, their sons and kinsmen, arrayed in fine linen, with cymbals, harps, and lyres, stood east of the altar with 120 priests who were trumpeters,

13 And it was the duty of the trumpeters and singers to make themselves heard in unison in praise and thanksgiving to YHWH — and when the song was raised, with trumpets and cymbals and other musical instruments, in praise to YHWH, For he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever — the house, the house of YHWH, was filled with a cloud,

14 So that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud, for the glory of YHWH filled the house of Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 5 is the covenant climactic moment of the Shelomoh narrative and of Divrei HaYamim: the covenant ark is installed in the covenant Most Holy Place, the covenant Levitical singers perform the covenant refrain — for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever — and the covenant glory of YHWH fills the covenant temple with a covenant cloud. The covenant presence of YHWH overwhelms the covenant sanctuary.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 13-14 — when the song was raised — for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever — the house was filled with a cloud, so that the priests could not stand to minister because of the glory of YHWH filled the house. The covenant music and the covenant divine presence are inseparable: the covenant song of covenant thanksgiving produces the covenant divine covenant response. YHWH inhabits the covenant praises of his covenant people.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 10 — there was nothing in the ark except the two tablets that Mosheh put there at Khoreiv, where YHWH made a covenant with the people of Yisra'EL. The covenant content of the covenant ark at the covenant temple dedication: only the covenant tablets of the covenant Sinai covenant — the covenant foundational covenant document — remain. The covenant manna jar and Aharon's staff — mentioned in Shemot and Bemidbar — are not mentioned here.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — all the priests who were present had consecrated themselves, without regard to their divisions. The covenant extraordinary covenant suspension of the covenant normal Davidic divisional system for the covenant dedication: every covenant available priest participates, not merely those assigned to that covenant week. The covenant dedication of the covenant temple is a covenant once-in-history covenant event that transcends the covenant normal covenant institutional covenant arrangements.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 6 Shelomoh Said YHWH Has Said He Would Dwell in Thick Darkness — Blessed Be YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL — The Prayer of Dedication — Hear From Heaven — When They Sin Against You — If They Return to You — Whatever Prayer Is Made

1 Then Shelomoh said, YHWH has said that he would dwell in thick darkness.

2 But I have built you an exalted house, a place for you to dwell in forever.

3 Then the king turned around and blessed all the assembly of Yisra'EL, while all the assembly of Yisra'EL stood.

4 And he said, Blessed be YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, who with his hand has fulfilled what he promised with his mouth to Dawid my father, saying,

5 Since the day that I brought my people out of the land of Egypt, I chose no city out of all the tribes of Yisra'EL in which to build a house, that my name might be there, and I chose no man as leader over my people Yisra'EL.

6 But I have chosen Yerushalayim that my name may be there, and I have chosen Dawid to be over my people Yisra'EL.

7 Now it was in the heart of Dawid my father to build a house for the name of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

8 But YHWH said to Dawid my father, Whereas it was in your heart to build a house for my name, you did well that it was in your heart.

9 Nevertheless, it is not you who shall build the house, but your son who shall be born to you shall build the house for my name.

10 Now YHWH has fulfilled his promise that he made. For I have risen in the place of Dawid my father and sit on the throne of Yisra'EL, as YHWH promised, and I have built the house for the name of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL.

11 And there I have set the ark, in which is the covenant of YHWH that he made with the people of Yisra'EL.

12 Then Shelomoh stood before the altar of YHWH in the presence of all the assembly of Yisra'EL and spread out his hands.

13 Shelomoh had made a bronze platform five cubits long, five cubits wide, and three cubits high, and had set it in the court, and he stood on it. Then he knelt on his knees in the presence of all the assembly of Yisra'EL, and spread out his hands toward heaven.

14 And said, O YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL, there is no Creator like you, in heaven or on earth, keeping covenant and showing steadfast love to your servants who walk before you with all their heart.

15 You have kept with your servant Dawid my father what you declared to him. You spoke with your mouth, and with your hand have fulfilled it this day.

16 Now therefore, O YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL, keep for your servant Dawid my father what you have promised him, saying, You shall not lack a man to sit before me on the throne of Yisra'EL, if only your sons pay close attention to their way, to walk in my Torah as you have walked before me.

17 Now therefore, O YHWH, Creator of Yisra'EL, let your word be confirmed, which you have spoken to your servant Dawid.

18 But will Creator indeed dwell with man on the earth? Behold, heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain you. How much less this house that I have built.

19 Yet have regard to the prayer of your servant and to his plea, O YHWH my Creator, listening to the cry and to the prayer that your servant prays before you,

20 That your eyes may be open day and night toward this house, the place where you have promised to set your name, that you may listen to the prayer that your servant offers toward this place.

21 And listen to the pleas of your servant and of your people Yisra'EL, when they pray toward this place. And listen from heaven your dwelling place, and when you hear, forgive.

22 If a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath and comes and swears his oath before your altar in this house,

23 Then hear from heaven and act and judge your servants, repaying the guilty by bringing his conduct on his own head, and vindicating the righteous by rewarding him according to his righteousness.

24 If your people Yisra'EL are defeated before the enemy because they have sinned against you, and they turn again and acknowledge your name and pray and plead with you in this house,

25 Then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of your people Yisra'EL and bring them back to the land that you gave to them and to their fathers.

26 When heaven is shut up and there is no rain because they have sinned against you, if they pray toward this place and acknowledge your name and turn from their sin, when you afflict them,

27 Then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of your servants, your people Yisra'EL, when you teach them the good way in which they should walk, and grant rain upon your land, which you have given to your people as an inheritance.

28 If there is famine in the land, if there is pestilence or blight or mildew or locust or caterpillar, if their enemies besiege them in the land at their gates, whatever plague, whatever sickness there is,

29 Whatever prayer, whatever plea is made by any man or by all your people Yisra'EL, each knowing his own affliction and his own sorrow and stretching out his hands toward this house,

30 Then hear from heaven your dwelling place and forgive and render to each whose heart you know, according to all his ways, for you, you only, know the hearts of the children of mankind,

31 That they may fear you and walk in your ways all the days that they live in the land that you gave to our fathers.

32 Likewise, when a foreigner, who is not of your people Yisra'EL, comes from a far country for the sake of your great name and your mighty hand and your outstretched arm, when he comes and prays toward this house,

33 Hear from heaven your dwelling place and do according to all for which the foreigner calls to you, in order that all the peoples of the earth may know your name and fear you, as do your people Yisra'EL, and that they may know that this house that I have built is called by your name.

34 If your people go out to battle against their enemies, by whatever way you shall send them, and they pray to you toward this city that you have chosen and the house that I have built for your name,

35 Then hear from heaven their prayer and their plea, and maintain their cause.

36 If they sin against you — for there is no one who does not sin — and you are angry with them and give them to an enemy, so that they are carried away captive to a land far or near,

37 Yet if they turn their heart in the land to which they have been carried captive, and repent and plead with you in the land of their captivity, saying, We have sinned and have acted perversely and wickedly,

38 If they repent with all their mind and with all their heart in the land of their captivity to which they were carried captive, and pray toward their land, which you gave to their fathers, the city that you have chosen, and the house that I have built for your name,

39 Then hear from heaven your dwelling place their prayer and their pleas, and maintain their cause and forgive your people who have sinned against you.

40 Now, O my Creator, let your eyes be open and your ears attentive to the prayer of this place.

41 And now arise, O YHWH Creator, and go to your resting place, you and the ark of your might. Let your priests, O YHWH Creator, be clothed with salvation, and let your saints rejoice in your goodness.

42 O YHWH Creator, do not turn away the face of your anointed one. Remember your steadfast love for Dawid your servant.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 6 contains the longest prayer in the Hebrew covenant historical narrative — Shelomoh's covenant dedication prayer — covering every major covenant crisis the covenant community might face: covenant judicial covenant disputes, covenant military covenant defeat, covenant drought, covenant famine, covenant pestilence, covenant exile. The covenant prayer is a covenant comprehensive covenant intercession that anticipates the entire subsequent covenant history of the covenant community.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 32-33 — when a foreigner who is not of your people Yisra'EL comes from a far country for the sake of your great name — hear from heaven and do according to all for which the foreigner calls to you, in order that all the peoples of the earth may know your name. The covenant universalism of the covenant temple's covenant prayer: the covenant temple is a covenant house of covenant prayer for all covenant peoples, not only the covenant community of Yisra'EL.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 36-39 — if they sin against you — and you are angry and give them to an enemy — yet if they turn their heart in the land of their captivity and repent — then hear from heaven their prayer and pleas. The covenant exile petition embedded within the covenant temple dedication prayer: Shelomoh at the covenant height of covenant national covenant blessing prays for the covenant community's covenant restoration from the covenant exile that has not yet occurred.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18 — will Creator indeed dwell with man on the earth? Behold, heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain you. How much less this house that I have built. The covenant theological precision of Shelomoh's covenant prayer: the covenant king building the covenant temple acknowledges that the covenant temple cannot contain YHWH. The covenant temple is not a covenant house that restricts YHWH but a covenant place where YHWH's covenant name is set and toward which covenant prayer is directed.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 7 Fire Came Down From Heaven — The Glory of YHWH Filled the Temple — The Priests Could Not Enter — All the People of Yisra'EL Bowed Down — YHWH Appeared to Shelomoh — If My People Humble Themselves

1 As soon as Shelomoh finished his prayer, fire came down from heaven and consumed the burnt offering and the sacrifices, and the glory of YHWH filled the temple.

2 And the priests could not enter the house of YHWH, because the glory of YHWH filled YHWH's house.

3 When all the people of Yisra'EL saw the fire come down and the glory of YHWH on the temple, they bowed down with their faces to the ground on the pavement and worshiped and gave thanks to YHWH, saying, For he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever.

4 Then the king and all the people offered sacrifice before YHWH.

5 King Shelomoh offered as a sacrifice 22,000 oxen and 120,000 sheep. So the king and all the people dedicated the house of Creator.

6 The priests stood at their posts; the Levites also, with the instruments for music to YHWH that King Dawid had made for giving thanks to YHWH — for his steadfast love endures forever — whenever Dawid offered praises by their ministry. Opposite them the priests sounded trumpets, and all Yisra'EL stood.

7 And Shelomoh consecrated the middle of the court that was before the house of YHWH, for there he offered the burnt offering and the fat of the peace offerings, because the bronze altar that Shelomoh had made could not hold the burnt offering and the grain offering and the fat.

8 At that time Shelomoh held the feast for seven days, and all Yisra'EL with him, a very great assembly, from Levo-Hamat to the Brook of Egypt.

9 And on the eighth day they held a solemn assembly, for they had kept the dedication of the altar seven days and the feast seven days.

10 On the twenty-third day of the seventh month he sent the people away to their homes, joyful and glad of heart for the prosperity that YHWH had granted to Dawid and to Shelomoh and to Yisra'EL his people.

11 Thus Shelomoh finished the house of YHWH and the king's house. All that Shelomoh had planned to do in the house of YHWH and in his own house he successfully accomplished.

12 Then YHWH appeared to Shelomoh in the night and said to him, I have heard your prayer and have chosen this place for myself as a house of sacrifice.

13 When I shut up the heavens so that there is no rain, or command the locust to devour the land, or send pestilence among my people,

14 If my people who are called by my name humble themselves, and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven and will forgive their sin and heal their land.

15 Now my eyes will be open and my ears attentive to the prayer that is made in this place.

16 For now I have chosen and consecrated this house that my name may be there forever. My eyes and my heart will be there for all time.

17 And as for you, if you walk before me as Dawid your father walked, doing according to all that I have commanded you and keeping my statutes and my rules,

18 Then I will establish your royal throne, as I covenanted with Dawid your father, saying, You shall not lack a man to rule Yisra'EL.

19 But if you turn aside and forsake my statutes and my commandments that I have set before you, and go and serve other gods and worship them,

20 Then I will pluck you up from my land that I have given you, and this house that I have consecrated for my name, I will cast out of my sight, and I will make it a proverb and a byword among all peoples.

21 And at this house, which was exalted, everyone passing by will be astonished and say, Why has YHWH done thus to this land and to this house?

22 Then they will say, Because they abandoned YHWH, the Creator of their fathers who brought them out of the land of Egypt, and laid hold on other gods and worshiped them and served them. Therefore he has brought all this disaster on them.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 7 is the covenant divine response to the covenant dedication prayer: fire from heaven consumes the covenant offerings — the covenant sign of covenant divine acceptance — and YHWH appears to Shelomoh at night with the covenant foundational promise that contains the most frequently cited verse in the book: if my people who are called by my name humble themselves and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways — then I will hear from heaven and forgive their sin and heal their land.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 14 — if my people who are called by my name humble themselves, and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven and will forgive their sin and heal their land. The covenant fourfold covenant condition: humility — prayer — seeking — turning. The covenant community's covenant restoration requires covenant active covenant engagement on four covenant levels simultaneously, not merely one.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 19-22 — if you turn aside and forsake my statutes — I will pluck you up from my land — and this house — I will cast out of my sight — and at this house, everyone passing by will be astonished. The covenant warning embedded within the covenant dedication: the covenant house consecrated for YHWH's covenant name can become a covenant witness against the covenant community that abandoned the covenant. The covenant temple is not an unconditional covenant guarantee.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — fire came down from heaven and consumed the burnt offering and the sacrifices. The covenant fire from heaven is the covenant same covenant sign that authenticated Eliyahu's covenant offering at Karmel — Melakhim Aleph 18:38 — and Dawid's covenant offering at Ornan's threshing floor — Divrei HaYamim Aleph 21:26. The covenant fire from heaven is the covenant divine covenant acceptance signal that requires no covenant human covenant interpretation.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 8 At the End of Twenty Years — The Cities That Khuram Had Given — Shelomoh Subjected the Remaining Peoples — The Daughter of Pharaoh — The Regular Order of the Priests — The Offerings at the Appointed Festivals

1 At the end of twenty years, in which Shelomoh had built the house of YHWH and his own house,

2 Shelomoh rebuilt the cities that Khuram had given to him, and settled the people of Yisra'EL in them.

3 And Shelomoh went to Hamat-Tzovah and took it.

4 He built Tadmor in the wilderness and all the store cities that he built in Hamat.

5 He also built Upper Beit-Khoron and Lower Beit-Khoron, fortified cities with walls, gates, and bars,

6 And Ba'alat and all the store cities that Shelomoh had and all the cities for his chariots and the cities for his horsemen, and whatever Shelomoh desired to build in Yerushalayim, in Lebanon, and in all the land of his dominion.

7 All the people who were left of the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Yevusites, who were not of Yisra'EL,

8 From their descendants who were left after them in the land, whom the people of Yisra'EL had not destroyed — these Shelomoh drafted as forced labor, and so they are to this day.

9 But of the people of Yisra'EL Shelomoh made no slaves for his work; they were soldiers, and his officers, the commanders of his chariots, and his horsemen.

10 And these were the chief officers of King Shelomoh, 250, who exercised authority over the people.

11 Shelomoh brought Pharaoh's daughter up from the city of Dawid to the house that he had built for her, for he said, My wife shall not live in the house of Dawid king of Yisra'EL, for the places to which the ark of YHWH has come are holy.

12 Then Shelomoh offered up burnt offerings to YHWH on the altar of YHWH that he had built before the vestibule,

13 As the duty of each day required, offering according to the commandment of Mosheh for the Shabbatot, the new moons, and the three annual feasts — the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the Feast of Weeks, and the Feast of Booths.

14 According to the ruling of Dawid his father, he appointed the divisions of the priests for their service, and the Levites for their offices of praise and ministry before the priests as the duty of each day required, and the gatekeepers in their divisions at each gate, for so Dawid the man of Creator had commanded.

15 And they did not turn aside from what the king had commanded the priests and Levites concerning any matter and concerning the treasuries.

16 Thus was accomplished all the work of Shelomoh from the day the foundation of the house of YHWH was laid until it was finished. So the house of YHWH was completed.

17 Then Shelomoh went to Etzyon-Gever and Elot on the shore of the sea, in the land of Edom.

18 And Khuram sent to him by the hand of his servants ships and servants familiar with the sea, and they went to Ofir together with the servants of Shelomoh and brought from there 450 talents of gold and brought it to King Shelomoh.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 8 documents the covenant completion of the covenant building program and the covenant establishment of the covenant regular covenant worship according to the covenant Mosaic covenant calendar and the covenant Davidic covenant institutional order. The covenant worship cycle of Shabbat, new moon, and three annual feasts — verse 13 — operates in the covenant newly completed covenant temple exactly as the covenant Torah commanded.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 11 — my wife shall not live in the house of Dawid king of Yisra'EL, for the places to which the ark of YHWH has come are holy. Shelomoh's covenant awareness that Pharaoh's covenant daughter — a covenant non-covenant woman — cannot dwell in the covenant space that has been covenant consecrated by the covenant ark's covenant presence. The covenant holiness of the covenant city constrains even the covenant king's covenant household arrangements.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 14-15 — according to the ruling of Dawid his father, he appointed the divisions of the priests for their service and the Levites for their offices — and they did not turn aside from what the king had commanded. The covenant institutional covenant obedience of the covenant Levitical and covenant priestly orders: they execute the covenant Davidic covenant organizational structure with covenant complete covenant faithfulness.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16 — thus was accomplished all the work of Shelomoh from the day the foundation of the house of YHWH was laid until it was finished. The covenant narrative of the covenant temple building has now reached its covenant conclusion. The covenant compiler's theological statement: the covenant building project initiated by Dawid's covenant heart and executed by Shelomoh's covenant hands is covenant completely accomplished. The covenant promise of Divrei HaYamim Aleph 17 is covenant fulfilled.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 9 The Queen of Sheva Heard the Fame of Shelomoh — She Tested Him With Hard Questions — King Shelomoh Surpassed All the Kings — The Weight of Gold That Came to Shelomoh — His Royal Throne — Shelomoh Reigned Over All the Kings — He Died

1 Now when the queen of Sheva heard of the fame of Shelomoh, she came to Yerushalayim to test him with hard questions, having a very great retinue and camels bearing spices and very much gold and precious stones. And when she came to Shelomoh, she told him all that was on her mind.

2 And Shelomoh answered all her questions. There was nothing hidden from Shelomoh that he could not explain to her.

3 And when the queen of Sheva had seen the wisdom of Shelomoh, the house that he had built,

4 The food of his table, the seating of his officials, and the attendance of his servants, and their clothing, his cupbearers, and their clothing, and his burnt offerings that he offered at the house of YHWH, there was no more breath in her.

5 And she said to the king, The report was true that I heard in my own land of your words and of your wisdom,

6 But I did not believe the reports until I came and my own eyes had seen it. And behold, half the greatness of your wisdom was not told me. You surpass the report that I heard.

7 Happy are your men. Happy are your servants, who continually stand before you and hear your wisdom.

8 Blessed be YHWH your Creator, who has delighted in you and set you on his throne as king for YHWH your Creator. Because your Creator loved Yisra'EL and would establish them forever, he has made you king over them, that you may execute justice and righteousness.

9 Then she gave the king 120 talents of gold, and a very great quantity of spices, and precious stones. There were no spices such as those that the queen of Sheva gave to King Shelomoh.

10 Moreover, the servants of Khuram and the servants of Shelomoh, who brought gold from Ofir, also brought algum wood and precious stones.

11 And the king made from the algum wood steps for the house of YHWH and for the king's house, lyres also and harps for the singers. There were none such as these seen before in the land of Yehudah.

12 And King Shelomoh gave to the queen of Sheva all that she desired, whatever she asked besides what she had brought to the king. So she turned and went back to her own land with her servants.

13 Now the weight of gold that came to Shelomoh in one year was 666 talents of gold,

14 Besides that which the explorers and merchants brought. And all the kings of Arabia and the governors of the land brought gold and silver to Shelomoh.

15 King Shelomoh made 200 large shields of beaten gold. Six hundred shekels of beaten gold went into each shield.

16 And he made 300 shields of beaten gold. Three hundred shekels of gold went into each shield, and the king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.

17 The king also made a great ivory throne and overlaid it with pure gold.

18 The throne had six steps and a footstool of gold, which were attached to the throne, and on each side of the seat were armrests and two lions standing beside the armrests,

19 While twelve lions stood there, one on each end of a step on the six steps. The like of it was never made in any kingdom.

20 All King Shelomoh's drinking vessels were of gold, and all the vessels of the House of the Forest of Lebanon were of pure gold. Silver was not considered as anything in the days of Shelomoh.

21 For the king's ships went to Tarshish with the servants of Khuram. Once every three years the ships of Tarshish used to come bringing gold, silver, ivory, apes, and peacocks.

22 Thus King Shelomoh excelled all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom.

23 And all the kings of the earth sought the presence of Shelomoh to hear his wisdom, which Creator had put into his mind.

24 Every one of them brought his present, articles of silver and of gold, garments, myrrh, spices, horses, and mules, so much year by year.

25 And Shelomoh had 4,000 stalls for horses and chariots, and 12,000 horsemen, whom he stationed in the chariot cities and with the king in Yerushalayim.

26 And he ruled over all the kings from the Euphrates to the land of the Pelishtim and to the border of Egypt.

27 The king made silver as common in Yerushalayim as stone, and he made cedar as plentiful as the sycamore of the Shephelah.

28 And horses were imported for Shelomoh from Egypt and from all lands.

29 Now the rest of the acts of Shelomoh, from first to last, are they not written in the history of Natan the prophet, and in the prophecy of Ahiyah the Shilonite, and in the visions of Iddo the seer concerning Yarov'am the son of Nevat?

30 Shelomoh reigned in Yerushalayim over all Yisra'EL forty years.

31 And Shelomoh slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of Dawid his father, and Rekhavam his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 9 is the covenant climax of the Shelomoh narrative — the covenant visit of the queen of Sheva — a covenant ruler from the covenant Afro-Asiatic covenant corridor of the northeast African covenant geographic region — who comes to test Shelomoh's covenant wisdom and departs acknowledging the blessing of YHWH on Shelomoh's covenant kingdom. The covenant compiler's Shelomoh narrative ends with this covenant universal acknowledgment of covenant Davidic covenant greatness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 8 — blessed be YHWH your Creator, who has delighted in you and set you on his throne as king for YHWH your Creator. Because your Creator loved Yisra'EL and would establish them forever, he has made you king over them, that you may execute justice and righteousness. The queen of Sheva's covenant theological interpretation of Shelomoh's kingship: the covenant king exists to execute covenant justice and covenant righteousness for the covenant people that YHWH loves.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 9 in full. The Divrei HaYamim account of Shelomoh differs significantly from Melakhim: the covenant compiler of Divrei HaYamim omits Shelomoh's covenant apostasy — Melakhim Aleph 11 — entirely. The covenant Shelomoh of Divrei HaYamim is the covenant ideal temple-builder and covenant wisdom-king whose covenant reign is the covenant golden age from which the covenant community draws covenant inspiration.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — the queen of Sheva came from a far country to test Shelomoh with hard questions and departed acknowledging his wisdom and blessing YHWH. The covenant narrative of the queen's covenant visit is the covenant fulfillment of the covenant temple dedication prayer: that all the peoples of the earth may know your name — Chapter 6:33. The covenant wisdom of Shelomoh draws the covenant nations to YHWH without covenant military conquest — covenant wisdom achieves what covenant armies cannot.

THE KINGS OF YEHUDAH — CHAPTERS 10 THROUGH 18

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 10 Rekhavam Went to Shekhem — Yarov'am Returned From Egypt — Your Father Made Our Yoke Heavy — My Father Disciplined You With Whips — The King Did Not Listen — Yisra'EL Departed to Their Tents

1 Rekhavam went to Shekhem, for all Yisra'EL had come to Shekhem to make him king.

2 And as soon as Yarov'am the son of Nevat heard of it — for he was in Egypt, where he had fled from King Shelomoh — Yarov'am returned from Egypt.

3 And they sent and called him. And Yarov'am and all Yisra'EL came and said to Rekhavam,

4 Your father made our yoke heavy. Now therefore lighten the hard service of your father and his heavy yoke on us, and we will serve you.

5 He said to them, Come to me again in three days. So the people went away.

6 Then King Rekhavam took counsel with the old men, who had stood before Shelomoh his father while he was yet alive, saying, How do you advise me to answer this people?

7 And they said to him, If you will be good to this people and please them and speak good words to them, then they will be your servants forever.

8 But he abandoned the counsel that the old men gave him, and took counsel with the young men who had grown up with him and stood before him.

9 And he said to them, What do you advise that we answer this people who have said to me, Lighten the yoke that your father put on us?

10 And the young men who had grown up with him said to him, Thus shall you speak to the people who said to you, Your father made our yoke heavy, but you lighten it for us. Thus shall you say to them, My little finger is thicker than my father's thigh.

11 And now, whereas my father laid on you a heavy yoke, I will add to your yoke. My father disciplined you with whips, but I will discipline you with scorpions.

12 So Yarov'am and all the people came to Rekhavam the third day, as the king had said, Come to me again the third day.

13 And the king answered them harshly. And King Rekhavam abandoned the counsel of the old men

14 And spoke to them according to the counsel of the young men, saying, My father made your yoke heavy, but I will add to it. My father disciplined you with whips, but I will discipline you with scorpions.

15 So the king did not listen to the people, for it was a turn of affairs brought about by Creator that YHWH might fulfill his word, which he spoke by Ahiyah the Shilonite to Yarov'am the son of Nevat.

16 And when all Yisra'EL saw that the king did not listen to them, the people answered the king, What portion have we in Dawid? We have no inheritance in the son of Yishai. Each of you to your tents, O Yisra'EL. Look now to your own house, Dawid. So all Yisra'EL went to their tents.

17 But Rekhavam reigned over the people of Yisra'EL who lived in the cities of Yehudah.

18 Then King Rekhavam sent Hadoram, who was taskmaster over the forced labor, and the people of Yisra'EL stoned him to death with stones. And King Rekhavam quickly mounted his chariot to flee to Yerushalayim.

19 So Yisra'EL has been in rebellion against the house of Dawid to this day.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 10 is the covenant rupture — the covenant division of the covenant kingdom that the covenant compiler presents as covenant divine covenant fulfillment — verse 15 — of Ahiyah's covenant prophecy rather than merely covenant political covenant folly. Rekhavam's covenant rejection of covenant elder covenant wisdom in favor of covenant youthful covenant pride is the covenant human instrument of the covenant divine covenant purpose.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 7 — if you will be good to this people and please them and speak good words to them, then they will be your servants forever. The covenant counsel of the covenant elders is the covenant counsel of covenant servant-leadership: the covenant king who serves the covenant people receives covenant loyalty. The covenant counsel rejected by Rekhavam is the covenant counsel of the covenant book of Proverbs that his father wrote.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 15 — the king did not listen to the people, for it was a turn of affairs brought about by Creator. The covenant theological interpretation of the covenant political disaster: the covenant hardening of Rekhavam's covenant heart is the covenant instrument of YHWH's covenant fulfillment of the covenant prophetic covenant word. The covenant human covenant folly and the covenant divine covenant purpose are the covenant same covenant event.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16 — what portion have we in Dawid? We have no inheritance in the son of Yishai. The covenant political slogan of covenant northern covenant independence repeats the covenant words of Sheva the son of Bikhri in Shemu'el Bet 20:1. The covenant northern covenant tribes have been accumulating covenant resentment toward the covenant Davidic house — Rekhavam's covenant harshness releases what was already under covenant pressure.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 11 documents the covenant obedience of Rekhavam to the covenant prophetic covenant word: when Shemayah commands him not to fight against the covenant northern tribes, Rekhavam listens — a covenant positive covenant characteristic the covenant compiler notes. The covenant migration of the covenant Levites and covenant priests to the covenant south because of Yarov'am's covenant apostasy is the covenant providential covenant consolidation of covenant faithful worship in Yehudah.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 16 — those who had set their hearts to seek YHWH Creator of Yisra'EL came after the Levites from all the tribes of Yisra'EL to Yerushalayim to sacrifice to YHWH. The covenant voluntary covenant migration of covenant faithful Israelites from the northern covenant tribes to Yehudah: those whose covenant hearts are set toward YHWH follow the covenant Levites southward. The covenant division of the covenant kingdom does not divide the covenant faithful.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 17 — for three years they walked in the way of Dawid and Shelomoh. The covenant brief covenant faithful period of Rekhavam's reign: three years of covenant following the covenant Davidic covenant pattern. The covenant compiler documents this covenant positive period before the covenant subsequent covenant failure of Chapter 12 — the covenant history of every covenant king includes both covenant faithful and covenant faithless covenant episodes.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — this thing is from me. The covenant divine covenant ownership of the covenant division: YHWH himself declares that the covenant division of the covenant kingdom is his covenant act. The covenant compiler's covenant theological interpretation prevents the covenant community from attributing the covenant division solely to covenant human covenant failure. The covenant division is covenant simultaneously covenant consequential and covenant divinely covenant purposed.

DIVREI HAYAMIM BET — CHAPTER 12 Rekhavam Abandoned the Torah — Shishak King of Egypt Came Up — Shemayah Said You Abandoned Me — They Humbled Themselves — YHWH Relented — Shishak Took the Treasuries — Rekhavam Did Evil

2 In the fifth year of King Rekhavam, because they had been unfaithful to YHWH, Shishak king of Egypt came up against Yerushalayim

12 And the messenger who went to summon Mikha'yahu said to him, Behold, the words of the prophets with one accord are favorable to the king. Let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak favorably.

13 But Mikha'yahu said, As YHWH lives, what my Creator says, that I will speak.

14 And when he had come to the king, the king said to him, Mikha'yahu, shall we go to Ramot-Gil'ad to battle, or shall I refrain? And he answered, Go up and triumph. They will be given into your hand.

15 But the king said to him, How many times shall I make you swear that you speak to me nothing but the truth in the name of YHWH?

16 And he said, I saw all Yisra'EL scattered on the mountains, as sheep that have no shepherd. And YHWH said, These have no master. Let each return to his home in peace.

17 And the king of Yisra'EL said to Yehoshafat, Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy good concerning me, but evil?

18 And Mikha'yahu said, Therefore hear the word of YHWH: I saw YHWH sitting on his throne, and all the host of heaven standing on his right hand and on his left.

19 And YHWH said, Who will entice Akhav the king of Yisra'EL, that he may go up and fall at Ramot-Gil'ad? And one said one thing, and another said another.

20 Then a spirit came forward and stood before YHWH, saying, I will entice him. And YHWH said to him, By what means?

21 And he said, I will go out, and will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, You are to entice him, and you shall succeed. Go out and do so.

22 Now therefore behold, YHWH has put a lying spirit in the mouth of these your prophets. YHWH has declared disaster for you.

23 Then Tzidkiyahu the son of Kena'anah came near and struck Mikha'yahu on the cheek and said, Which way did the Spirit of YHWH go from me to speak to you?

24 And Mikha'yahu said, Behold, you shall see on that day when you go into an inner chamber to hide yourself.

25 And the king of Yisra'EL said, Seize Mikha'yahu and take him back to Amon the governor of the city and to Yo'ash the king's son,

26 And say, Thus says the king, Put this fellow in prison and feed him with meager rations of bread and water until I return in peace.

27 And Mikha'yahu said, If you return in peace, YHWH has not spoken by me. Hear, all you peoples.

28 So the king of Yisra'EL and Yehoshafat the king of Yehudah went up to Ramot-Gil'ad.

29 And the king of Yisra'EL said to Yehoshafat, I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your robes. So the king of Yisra'EL disguised himself, and they went into battle.

30 Now the king of Syria had commanded the captains of his chariots, Fight with neither small nor great, but only with the king of Yisra'EL.

31 And when the captains of the chariots saw Yehoshafat, they said, It is the king of Yisra'EL. So they turned to fight against him. And Yehoshafat cried out, and YHWH helped him. Creator drew them away from him.

32 For when the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Yisra'EL, they turned back from pursuing him.

33 But a certain man drew his bow at random and struck the king of Yisra'EL between the scale armor and the breastplate. Therefore he said to the driver of his chariot, Turn around and carry me out of the battle, for I am wounded.

34 And the battle continued that day, and the king of Yisra'EL was propped up in his chariot facing the Syrians until evening. Then at sunset he died.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Orit Ge'ez and Masoretic are the primary authorities. Chapter 18 is the covenant narrative of Yehoshafat's covenant fatal covenant alliance with Akhav — the covenant great covenant warning within the covenant Yehoshafat narrative. The covenant alliance with the covenant northern covenant apostasy brings Yehoshafat into covenant danger: he nearly dies at Ramot-Gil'ad because he has been drawn into Akhav's covenant war. The covenant cry of verse 31 — YHWH helped him — is the covenant only thing that saves Yehoshafat's covenant life.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 13 — as YHWH lives, what my Creator says, that I will speak. Mikha'yahu's covenant declaration before the covenant court of Akhav is the covenant confession of the true covenant prophet: the covenant word of YHWH is the covenant sole covenant authority over the covenant prophet's covenant speech, regardless of covenant royal covenant pressure. The covenant true covenant prophet speaks YHWH's covenant word even when the covenant king hates it.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic text preserves Chapter 18 in full. Verse 18-22 — the covenant heavenly vision of Mikha'yahu: YHWH enthroning, the covenant host of heaven assembled, the covenant decision to send a covenant lying spirit to the covenant false prophets. The covenant sovereign determination of covenant historical outcomes — even through covenant deception of covenant false covenant prophets — is the covenant theological statement: YHWH's covenant purposes are not thwarted by covenant human covenant resistance, even when that resistance takes the form of covenant prophetic covenant proclamation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31 — Yehoshafat cried out, and YHWH helped him. Creator drew them away from him. The covenant moment of covenant grace within the covenant narrative of covenant alliance with covenant apostasy: Yehoshafat's covenant cry to YHWH in the middle of the covenant battle he should not have been in produces covenant divine covenant rescue. The covenant compiler documents the covenant grace that extracts the covenant faithful king from the covenant consequences of covenant poor covenant judgment.
